antipattern.symptom: 3-model MVP ships with 47 packages, ports/adapters folders, ABC-based repositories, and the dev team spends 3x the planned time fighting the layering.
antipattern.root-cause: Team picked "clean-arch" because it sounded mature. Forbidden by rule r3-pick-layering when model_count < 10.
antipattern.fix: Pick "simple" (fat models + thin views). Re-evaluate at the 12-month checkpoint or when model_count crosses 10. Spec validator forbids clean-arch under the threshold.

antipattern.symptom: 200-model SaaS has business logic in 4000-line model methods. Onboarding a developer takes 6 months. Tests are slow and fragile.
antipattern.root-cause: "simple" layering was chosen at year-1 and never revisited. Model count grew past 50 without any service layer being introduced.
antipattern.fix: Trigger refactor checkpoint when model_count crosses 50 OR team_size crosses 10. Spec validator forbids "simple" when model_count >= 50.

antipattern.symptom: Django 5.2 upgrade fails because django-foo-bar (last commit 2022) hasn't migrated off `from django.conf.urls`.
antipattern.root-cause: Package was adopted without auditing recent_commits. Now blocks the upgrade and forces fork + maintain.
antipattern.fix: For every candidate dependency, run the 8-signal audit before adoption. Verdict "adopt" requires recent_commits=true AND no_known_cves=true. Sunset plan required for any high-risk verdict.

antipattern.symptom: Architecture re-debate happens every retrospective. Implementation stalls. Team morale drops.
antipattern.root-cause: The decision tree was re-run without a trigger event (12-month checkpoint, 50-model milestone, major refactor). Discussion is unbounded.
antipattern.fix: Define triggers in the record's metadata. Re-walk only when triggered. Spec field last_reviewed + a triggers field controls re-walk cadence.
